CheckTitle: Review service principals with high-privilege granted application permissions
Description: Identifies service principals that have been granted high-privilege application permissions (app roles) on Microsoft Graph, such as RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory, Application.ReadWrite.All, or Mail.ReadWrite. Unlike declared permissions on app registrations, these are actually consented and active.
Risk: Granted application permissions are live, consented access. A service principal with Application.ReadWrite.All can create new app registrations and grant itself more permissions. Mail.ReadWrite grants full mailbox access across all users. These represent the actual blast radius of a compromised workload identity.

Remediation.Recommendation: Audit each high-privilege app role assignment. Remove unnecessary permissions. Use application access policies to scope mailbox access. Prefer delegated permissions with user context where possible.
